evidence_scorecard.key_metric: R²=0.992 real vs 0.438 shuffled (z=10.3, p≈0)
evidence_scorecard.notes: 2000 permutations; p-value = 0/2000 (no null R² reached real R²)

evidence_scorecard.description: Positional model generalises to unseen seals (held-out)
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 97.7% accuracy, r=0.999/0.994 on blind 20%
evidence_scorecard.notes: 80/20 site split; model not overfit to training corpus

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 90% of H+M signs stable across 9 sites (mean=0.94)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Grammar is not a Harappa/Mohenjo-daro artifact — holds everywhere

evidence_scorecard.description: Bigram conditional entropy confirms sequential structure
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: H(X2|X1)/H(X1) ratio (NL expect 0.5-0.8; random=1.0)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Conditional entropy << marginal entropy confirms word-order structure

evidence_scorecard.description: Type-Token Ratio consistent with administrative corpus
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: TTR ≈ 0.056 (expected 0.02-0.10 for short-text admin seals)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Not a random symbol set; consistent with reused administrative vocabulary

evidence_scorecard.description: Sign frequency Zipf exponent consistent with control corpora
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Indus α=1.28; comparison with Meroitic, Old Hebrew, Dravidian
evidence_scorecard.notes: F10 SYSTEMATIC_GAP may be corpus-type artifact — controls needed

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Block entropy H_n/n confirmed linguistic range (Rao et al. 2009 Science)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Independent prior confirmation; our entropy analysis replicates Rao 2009

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Spearman r between freq rank and terminal rate (expected r < 0)
evidence_scorecard.notes: High-frequency signs should be terminal (case suffixes) in Dravidian SOV

evidence_scorecard.description: Dravidian LM outperforms Sanskrit at 157 H+M anchors
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 88% of 157 readings Dravidian-favored, Δ log-P = +4.1
evidence_scorecard.notes: Sanskrit LM provides systematically worse fit for all H+M readings

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Drv-exclusive markers (zh, L, N, R, geminates, -an/-al suffix)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Redesigned F3: looks for phonemes physically absent in Sanskrit, not just uncommon

evidence_scorecard.description: DEDR etymology support for HIGH-confidence readings
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 53% of HIGH readings have explicit DEDR citation; 100% phonologically compatible
evidence_scorecard.notes: DEDR references are specific; random Dravidian words would not all fit positional profiles

evidence_scorecard.description: Terminal signs match Dravidian case-suffix inventory
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: M047 (mīn/min fish), M267 (genitive particle), class-IV terminal signs
evidence_scorecard.notes: 8 HIGH-confidence terminal signs have DEDR-grounded Dravidian grammatical readings

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Grammar model explains 44.3% of positional variance (Phase-133 resolution)
evidence_scorecard.notes: ~44% explained variance is consistent with a partial grammar model on agglutinative language

evidence_scorecard.description: Vowel harmony test consistent with Dravidian
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Phase-61 within-reading harmony 94%; Phase-133 resolved methodology mismatch
evidence_scorecard.notes: Vowel harmony is a Dravidian property; test methodology mismatch required resolution

evidence_scorecard.description: Meluhhan personal names from Mesopotamian texts
evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 6/14 (43%) attested Meluhhan names phonologically plausible with H+M readings
evidence_scorecard.notes: Limited by: Akkadian phonological distortion, incomplete anchor coverage

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Shu-ilishu phonemes /su/, /i/, /li/ coverage vs H+M reading set
evidence_scorecard.notes: Best single external anchor: known Meluhhan interpreter, dated c.2020 BCE

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: KL=0.708 semantic divergence; Chanhu-daro maritime vs Rakhigarhi administrative
evidence_scorecard.notes: If readings are meaningful, different site types should show different semantic profiles

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Terminal sign positional profiles match Tamil-Brahmi grammatical morpheme positions
evidence_scorecard.notes: Independent confirmation from a historically attested Dravidian script

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 7 HIGH-confidence iconographic anchors (unicorn=ai, fish=min, etc.)
evidence_scorecard.notes: Iconographic evidence is independent of distributional analysis

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 7 signs with BOTH iconographic AND DEDR support; not distributional-only
evidence_scorecard.notes: M047=min(fish/meen), M267=genitive particle, M063=ai/unicorn, etc.

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 82 signs: DEDR rebus + distributional + positional evidence
evidence_scorecard.notes: Not independently verifiable without bilingual text; internally consistent

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: 3 positional classes with distinct profiles; matches Dravidian SOV morphology
evidence_scorecard.notes: TERMINAL = case suffixes; INITIAL = titles/determinatives; MEDIAL = content signs

evidence_scorecard.key_metric: Recurring 3-element formula identified in ~60% of multi-sign seals
evidence_scorecard.notes: Formula matches Dravidian onomastic patterns; consistent with seal function

open_items.why: Only irrefutable proof; without it the decipherment remains hypothesis
Preprint must go through domain experts before formal claim
Expand beyond 1670 Holdat seals for stronger positional profiles

open_items.item: F9 on raw CISI (Vol.1-3) — single-sign seals
open_items.why: CISI JSON only has 1 single-sign seal; raw Vol.1-3 has many more

open_items.item: F3 redesign: proper phone-pair exclusivity matrix
open_items.why: Current CV-skeleton test still uses markers that may overlap
Phase-137 partial; need proper short-inscription administrative corpus set
Phase-25f was limited; full DEDR coverage cross-validation would strengthen L06
Fish-sign polysemy: maritime corpus test needs Gulf deposit seals
New bilingual seals, longer inscriptions, or contact-zone artifacts
